mapping.a_element: frustration arising from competition combined with heterogeneities in economic agents
mapping.b_element: frustration/disorder in random-bond, random-field and spin-glass systems

mapping.a_element: rugged spin-glass landscape with many metastable states (long epochs of stasis interrupted by crises)
mapping.b_element: the energy landscape of a frustrated disordered system whose ground state is sought

brief_justification: Both invoke the same object -- spin-glass / frustrated disordered systems and their rugged energy landscapes -- but their transferable machinery differs: paper A is a conceptual/methodological argument using spin-glass phenomenology as a metaphor for endogenous market dynamics, backed by agent-based simulation, whereas paper B is an algorithmic toolkit (combinatorial optimization via network flows, max-flow/min-cost-flow, matching, branch-and-cut) for exactly computing ground states. The overlap is shared subject matter and vocabulary, not a role-for-role governing process.
